response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled generalist engineer with broad experience across web, mobile, and embedded systems, but lacks the core competencies this role demands. The position requires strong Java and Kotlin expertise, deep JVM knowledge, and hands-on experience with Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate — none of which appear meaningfully in this resume. His primary stack is TypeScript and Node.js. While he has peripheral Java usage at Altom and once contributed to a C-based IDE at National Instruments, neither constitutes the enterprise JVM tooling background the role requires.
strengths: Genuine interest in developer tooling demonstrated through open-source test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), CI/CD infrastructure work, and MCP code generators
Proven ability to build and operate high-throughput, complex systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby)
Active knowledge-sharer via workshops and conference talks, indicating strong communication and collaboration skills
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin proficiency; Java appears only in passing for robotic arms and mobile tooling at Altom, with no JVM depth demonstrated
Zero experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework — the central technical requirement of this role
No IntelliJ Platform, JetBrains SDK, or equivalent JVM-based IDE plugin development experience; the LabWindows/CVI work was C-based and brief

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
